name: pre_sales
timeline_note: Pre-Sales engagements run 4 weeks from qualification to POC decision. Discovery weeks 1-2, POC scoping weeks 3-4. All due dates computed as TODAY + due_date_offset_days.

default_custom_fields.note: All values are plain text strings — no enum GID lookup needed. Allowed values match the GCA_GTM_ field descriptions.

milestones.name: ♦ Opportunity Qualified — {company}
milestones.notes: Milestone: GCA_GTM_ARR Potential confirmed and logged. GCA_GTM_Vector Opportunity / Workload URL added. Opportunity officially in active Pre-Sales.

milestones.name: ♦ Discovery Complete — {company}
milestones.notes: Milestone: Technical discovery done. Requirements documented, stakeholders mapped, competitive landscape noted, security posture assessed.

milestones.name: ♦ POC Scope & Success Criteria Agreed — {company}
milestones.notes: Milestone: Customer and CE have agreed on pilot group size, POC duration, success metrics, exit criteria, and decision timeline. Ready to proceed to POC.

milestones.name: ♦ Decision: POC Confirmed / Closed — {company}
milestones.notes: Milestone: Final Pre-Sales decision made. If POC: GCA_GTM_Stage updated to POC, triggering Tier 2 project creation (Rule 4). If closed: GCA_GTM_Outcome set and task archived.

subtasks.name: [Day 1] GCA_GTM_ARR Potential confirmed & entered in Asana field
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | Set GCA_GTM_ARR Potential custom field on the parent opportunity task. Required for Goal 1 (Pipeline ARR) tracking. Estimate based on team size and license tier.

subtasks.name: [Day 1] GCA_GTM_Vector Opportunity / Workload URL added
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | Paste the CRM opportunity URL into the GCA_GTM_Vector Opportunity / Workload custom field on the parent task.

subtasks.name: [Day 5] Initial technical discovery call
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | Cover: current dev tooling, team size, pain points, GCA awareness level, key stakeholders, and decision process.

subtasks.name: [Day 7] Document requirements & current stack
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | Record: languages, IDEs, CI/CD pipeline, security posture, VPC/proxy requirements, compliance needs, and license footprint.

subtasks.name: [Day 10] Map requirements to GCA capabilities
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | Identify fit and gaps between customer requirements and GCA features. Note competitive context vs GitHub Copilot / Cursor / others.

subtasks.name: [Day 10] Identify stakeholders (sponsor / IT / security)
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | Map: economic buyer, technical sponsor, IT admin, security reviewer. Confirm decision-making process and timeline.

subtasks.name: [Day 12] Competitive landscape noted
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | Document competing tools in evaluation and CE differentiation strategy. Note if customer has existing licenses elsewhere.

subtasks.name: [Day 17] Assess security & compliance requirements
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | Validate GCA data residency, VPC-SC, audit logging, and approval requirements with customer IT/security team.

subtasks.name: [Day 21] POC scope & success criteria agreed
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE + Customer | Agree on: pilot group size (10-20 devs), duration (4-6 weeks), success metrics (GCA_GTM_WAU Adoption % target), exit criteria, and decision timeline.

subtasks.name: [Day 28] Decision: Start POC / No Interest / Stalled
subtasks.notes: Owner: CE | If POC confirmed: update GCA_GTM_Stage field to POC on the parent task (triggers Rule 4 auto-project creation). If no go: set GCA_GTM_Outcome = Closed-Lost or No Interest.
